experiment: B21: B2 + IFE-lite local-curvature/global-entropy channel enhancement
note: B21 keeps the B2 auxiliary losses and RBSG-lite unchanged, then inserts a lightweight IFE-style channel enhancement on the final decoder feature. It is designed for low-contrast X-ray texture discrimination and avoids adding more boundary/rim supervision, rim suppression, multi-scale gates, or post-mask residual rescue.

failure_reflection.boundary_rim_reinforcement: pre-rim, stronger rim/gate, and consistency-style variants tended to raise precision but lower recall
failure_reflection.context_or_rescue_modules: PPM/detail/wavelet-style additions did not consistently beat B2 on held-out test results
failure_reflection.design_implication: try a near-identity channel-only enhancement that complements B2's spatial prior instead of competing with it

design_references.title: Instructive Feature Enhancement for Dichotomous Medical Image Segmentation
design_references.adopted_idea: use local curvature and global entropy/information cues to enhance discriminative medical-image features

design_references.title: nnU-Net Revisited: A Call for Rigorous Validation in 3D Medical Image Segmentation
design_references.adopted_idea: make conservative architecture changes on top of a strong U-Net baseline

design_references.title: When CNNs Outperform Transformers and Mambas: Revisiting Deep Architectures for Dental Caries Segmentation
design_references.adopted_idea: favor lightweight CNN-compatible modules for dental caries radiographs

ife_lite.position: final decoder feature before B2 pre-boundary/pre-rim heads and RBSG-lite
ife_lite.local_score: mean absolute first-order feature differences as a curvature/texture proxy
ife_lite.global_score: 1 - normalized spatial entropy of absolute channel activations
ife_lite.mode: channel-only residual amplification; detached statistics; no extra spatial attention map
